title: Your English Journey Map: What You'll Learn This Year!

questions.question: What does "Contents" mean?
options: A list of the chapters or topics in a book, usually found at the beginning.
Stories that are invented and not based on real events or facts.
A period of time in the school year during which classes are held.
A section of the book focusing on a particular topic or skill.
questions.correct_answer: A list of the chapters or topics in a book, usually found at the beginning.
questions.explanation: "Contents" means: A list of the chapters or topics in a book, usually found at the beginning.

questions.question: What does "Term" mean?
options: A section of the book focusing on a particular topic or skill.
A period of time in the school year during which classes are held.
A list of the chapters or topics in a book, usually found at the beginning.
Stories that are invented and not based on real events or facts.
questions.correct_answer: A period of time in the school year during which classes are held.
questions.explanation: "Term" means: A period of time in the school year during which classes are held.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: The Contents page is your complete ______ to the entire book.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "The Contents page is your complete guide to the entire book."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Units cover different areas of English: fiction, non-fiction, and poetry.
Review sections are included to help you assess your understanding and progress.
The Contents page is your complete guide to the entire book.
The book is organised into three terms, each with specific units of study.
questions.correct_answer: The book is organised into three terms, each with specific units of study.
questions.explanation: Correct! "The book is organised into three terms, each with specific units of study." is one of the key things to remember.

A list of the chapters or topics in a book, usually found at the beginning.
